content: Image-reuse detection in cruisinginthewake requires BOTH MD5 (exact bytes) and a perceptual hash (dHash on 9x8 grayscale via sharp, Hamming threshold 8) to catch recompresses / crops / mirrors. MD5 alone is insufficient — first dHash run caught Costa Deliziosa being passed off as Celebrity Millennium (md5 b771780a vs 8f86067b, dHash distance < 8) and Costa Firenze as Celebrity Infinity. Operator's directive: 'It should hash every image, and check it, and it should visually examine every image looking for one to be a simple recrop of another.' Scanners: admin/scan-image-reuse.cjs (md5, fast, pre-commit-suitable) + admin/scan-image-recrops.cjs (dHash, ~44s on full corpus, periodic audit).
